target: data/sections.json part1 scorecard — preempt globe-assumptions objection
patch_location: The sc-sublabel text inside the 0 Survive Scrutiny card
current_text: After reviewing each claim against published data and the model's own equations, none produces a prediction that both derives from dome geometry and differs from standard physics. Many referenced observations are real — the verdicts address interpretation, not the data. What would change this?
replacement_html: After reviewing each claim against published data and the model's own equations, none produces a prediction that both derives from dome geometry and differs from standard physics. This review does not assume globe geometry is correct — the strongest arguments (Parts 2 and 2b) use only the dome's own stated parameters and equations to show internal contradictions that require no external frame of reference. Many referenced observations are real — the verdicts address interpretation, not the data. What would change this?
holes_addressed: Preempts "you're using globe assumptions" objection directly in scorecard sublabel
Points reader to Parts 2/2b as the self-refuting core that needs no external assumptions
Maintains existing language and links, only adds two sentences

anticipated_objections.objection: Parts 3-5 DO use external data (satellite measurements, GPS, Gaia). Claiming you don't assume globe is misleading.
anticipated_objections.response: The added text says "the strongest arguments" use dome-only parameters, not ALL arguments. Parts 3-5 are explicitly labeled as "external evidence" tiers in the roadmap. The dome's self-contradictions in Parts 2/2b are sufficient by themselves — everything else is confirmatory.

anticipated_objections.objection: Saying "does not assume globe geometry is correct" is disingenuous when you use IGRF, WGS84, and satellite data throughout.
anticipated_objections.response: We use published measurements, not the globe model per se. IGRF field measurements exist independently of any geometric model — they are empirical magnetometer readings at known locations. WGS84 is a coordinate system. The dome author himself uses these datasets as ground truth for his predictions.

kernel_of_truth: The dome defender's objection has genuine force: much of the review DOES reference globe-derived data (IGRF, WGS84, Gaia parallax distances). Acknowledging this honestly while pointing to the self-contradiction arguments as the un-objectionable core is stronger than pretending the entire review is assumption-free.
